evaluation_summary: Benchmark evaluation of /design skill pipeline on 10 yeast synthetic biology cases
methodology: Each case was run through the 4-phase design pipeline (KEGG pathway discovery, GEM host assessment, UniProt enzyme sourcing, FBA simulation). Agent results were compared against ground truth from published papers. No ground truth or web search was used during pipeline execution.

pathway_design.step_recall: Ground truth steps found by agent / total GT steps (Found/N)
pathway_design.step_precision: Correct agent steps / total agent steps (Correct/M)
pathway_design.path_selection: match = same pathway; reasonable_alternative = valid but different route; wrong = incorrect pathway

engineering_strategy.heterologous_recall: GT heterologous genes found / total GT heterologous genes
engineering_strategy.heterologous_precision: Valid agent heterologous genes / total agent heterologous genes
engineering_strategy.ko_recall: GT KO targets found / total GT KOs
engineering_strategy.oe_recall: GT OE targets found / total GT OEs
engineering_strategy.source_species_match: exact=1.0 / same_domain=0.7 / different=0.4, averaged across genes

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Agent correctly reconstructed the core isoflavonoid pathway (Phe to daidzein, 7 steps). Used an alternative route via daidzein->2'-hydroxydaidzein (skipping the formononetin/methylation step). Completely missed the 4-step tailoring module (PTR1 pterocarpan reductase, PT1 prenyltransferase, OC1 oxidative cyclase, DMT1 demethylase) which is the key discovery of the paper.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 6 NADPH, 1 ATP, 3 O2. Roughly correct for the 10-step agent pathway but incomplete for the full 16-step GT pathway.

heterologous_recall.notes: Found core isoflavonoid pathway enzymes (PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS+CHR, CHI, IFS, HID, I2'H, IFR, PTS). Missed: OMT, PTR, PT, OC, DMT (tailoring), TAL (supplementary entry), CPR is extra.

heterologous_precision.notes: All 11 agent genes are biologically valid and well-characterized enzymes.

ko_recall.notes: GT does not list explicit KOs. Agent proposed PDC1/5/6/ARO10 KOs which are reasonable for phenylpropanoid production.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: tHMG1 (mevalonate), IDI1 (DMAPP supply), ERG20 (FPP/DMAPP balance) - all for prenylation which agent missed. Agent OEs: ZWF1/GND2 (NADPH) relate to GT's NADPH enhancement strategy but not to specific gene targets.

source_species_match.breakdown: GT all from G. glabra. Agent: A. thaliana (PAL,C4H,4CL,CPR)=0.4x4; G. max (CHS+CHR)=0.7; M. sativa (CHI,IFR)=0.7x2; G. echinata (IFS,HIDM,I2'H,PTS)=1.0x4 (same genus). Average=0.70

step_recall.notes: Agent started from zymosterol (GT step 9), correctly covering the key heterologous conversion steps. If counting only the post-zymosterol heterologous steps (GT steps 10-13 = 4 steps), recall is 4/4 with alternatives. Full 13-step recall is lower because native MVA/post-squalene steps were not enumerated.

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Agent used Kandutsch-Russell pathway variant (DHCR24 first, then isomerase/desaturase, then DHCR7). GT uses a slightly different ordering. For side-chain cleavage: agent used mammalian CYP11A1+FDX1+FDXR; GT used plant CYP87A3+ATR (engineered). Both are valid P450 side-chain cleavage systems.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 6 NADPH, 4 O2. NADPH count is reasonable; the full steroid pathway requires extensive NADPH.

heterologous_recall.notes: Found: DHCR24 (diff source), DHCR7 (diff source), P450scc (CYP11A1 vs CYP87A3), electron transfer (FDX1/FDXR vs ADR/ADX). Missed: ATR reductase, GDH (NADPH recycling), FPK+PTA (PP pathway), EBP matches ERG2 function.

ko_recall.notes: Found ERG6. Missed ERG5. Both are essential for blocking competing ergosterol synthesis.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: tHMG1, IDI1, ERG2/ERG3. Agent found tHMG1. Also proposed ERG9, ERG20, ZWF1 which are reasonable but ERG9/ERG20 are not in GT OE list.

source_species_match.breakdown: DHCR24: H.sapiens vs G.gallus (same domain=0.7); DHCR7: H.sapiens vs B.taurus (same domain=0.7); CYP11A1 vs CYP87A3: mammal vs plant (different=0.4); FDX1/FDXR vs ADR/ADX: both mammalian (0.7x2). Average=0.64

cases.product: Short- and medium-chain fatty acids (C8, C10, C12)

step_recall.notes: GT uses heterologous metazoan FAS (mFAS) with KS domain mutations for chain-length control. Agent uses native yeast FAS + heterologous thioesterases. Fundamentally different engineering approaches. Only acetyl-CoA supply steps overlap.

step_precision.notes: Agent steps are biochemically valid for a thioesterase-based strategy, but don't match the GT mFAS approach. Some steps are redundant (multiple thioesterases).

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Agent chose thioesterase-based SMCFA production (a valid approach in the literature) but the GT paper specifically describes engineered metazoan FAS (mFAS) with KS G113 mutations for chain-length control. The approaches are fundamentally different.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 14 NADPH, 4 ATP. These numbers are correct for the FA elongation cycle but the agent's pathway mechanism differs from GT.

heterologous_recall.notes: GT heterologous gene: mFAS (metazoan FAS megasynthase with KS G113 mutations). Agent proposed: CvFatB1, UcFatB1, TesA' (thioesterases). Zero overlap - completely different strategy.

heterologous_precision.notes: Agent's thioesterases are all well-characterized, valid enzymes for SMCFA production, just not what the paper used.

ko_recall.notes: GT has no explicit KOs. Agent proposed POX1 and ELO1 KOs which are reasonable for thioesterase-based SMCFA production.

oe_recall.notes: GT strategy is mFAS expression, not native gene OE. Agent proposed ACC1, ZWF1, ACS1 OEs.

source_species_match.breakdown: No gene overlap between GT and agent. Agent sources (Cuphea plant, Umbellularia plant, E. coli) are entirely different from GT (metazoan FAS). Default=0.4

step_recall.notes: Agent found all 5 core biosynthetic steps (PAL->C4H->4CL->CHS+CHR->CHI). Missed 2 precursor supply steps (PEX-mediated peroxisome engineering for acetyl-CoA, ACC1 for malonyl-CoA). If counting only the core pathway: 5/5=1.0

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Core biosynthetic pathway is an exact match: Phe -> cinnamate -> 4-coumarate -> coumaroyl-CoA -> isoliquiritigenin -> liquiritigenin. The difference is in precursor supply (GT uses novel PEX peroxisome engineering; agent uses conventional OEs).
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 2 NADPH, 1 ATP, 1 O2. Correct for the core 5-step pathway.

heterologous_recall.notes: All core pathway enzymes found: PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS, CHR, CHI. Agent additionally includes CPR (valid auxiliary). GT also has PEX genes from Y. lipolytica (not counted in core heterologous count).

heterologous_precision.notes: All agent genes (PAL2, C4H, CPR, 4CL2, CHS1, CHR, CHI1) are valid. CPR is needed for C4H.

ko_recall.notes: GT has no explicit KOs listed. Agent proposed ARO10, PDC5, PDC6 KOs which are reasonable.

oe_recall.notes: GT implies ACC1 enhancement. Agent proposed ACC1 OE. Also ARO7, ARO4 which are reasonable additions. The main GT innovation (PEX engineering) is a novel platform strategy not an OE.

source_species_match.breakdown: GT doesn't specify enzyme sources for core pathway. Agent uses A. thaliana (PAL,C4H,4CL,CPR), G. max (CHS,CHR), M. sativa (CHI). All are standard plant sources commonly used in the field. Default same_domain=0.7

step_recall.notes: Agent found the core salidroside branch (Tyr->4-HPP->4-HPA->Tyrosol->Salidroside = 4 steps matching GT steps 3-5,7). Missed: steps 1-2 (native shikimate, reasonable omission), step 6 (HT branch via HpaB/HpaC), step 8 (tGuSUS1 for UDP-Glc supply). For salidroside-specific path: 4/4=1.0

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Core salidroside pathway is correct: Ehrlich pathway (Tyr->tyrosol) + glucosylation. Agent correctly identified the tyrosol intermediate. Missing the hydroxytyrosol branch and the sucrose synthase UDP-Glc supply strategy.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 1 NADH, 1 UDP-glucose. Correct for the 4-step pathway.

heterologous_recall.notes: GT heterologous: PaHpaB, EcHpaC, RrU8GT33, tGuSUS1. Agent found: UGT73B6 (functionally equivalent to RrU8GT33). Missed: HpaB/HpaC (HT branch), tGuSUS1 (UDP-Glc supply).

ko_recall.notes: GT KO: SUC2. Agent proposed TYRNFT (YDR403W) and GSY2 (YLR258W) instead. Different targets but reasonable for UDP-Glc conservation.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: ARO7/TYR1 (implied), tGuSUS1. Agent found TYR1. Also ARO4 and UGP1 (reasonable alternatives for precursor supply).

source_species_match.breakdown: UGT73B6 from R. sachalinensis vs RrU8GT33 from R. rosea: same genus Rhodiola = ~1.0

step_recall.notes: Perfect match. All 8 heme biosynthesis steps (HEM1-HEM4, HEM12-HEM15) correctly identified with matching substrates, products, EC numbers.

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Exact match. Agent correctly identified KEGG module M00868 (glycine -> heme) as the native 8-step pathway.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 4 O2, 1 Fe2+, 8 succinyl-CoA, 8 glycine. Correct.

heterologous_recall.notes: Both GT and agent correctly identify that no heterologous genes are needed (entire pathway is native).

ko_recall.notes: GT KO: HMX1 (heme oxygenase, prevents heme -> biliverdin). Agent KO: COX10 (heme O synthase, prevents protoheme -> heme O). Both target heme degradation but via different routes. Agent's COX10 is a reasonable alternative anti-degradation target.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: HEM2, HEM3, HEM12, HEM13. Agent found: HEM12, HEM13. Also proposed HEM1 (reasonable but not in GT). Missed: HEM2 (PBG synthase), HEM3 (HMB synthase).

source_species_match.breakdown: No heterologous genes in either GT or agent - all native pathway.

step_recall.notes: All 7 GT steps covered. Agent splits GT step 1 (PAL/TAL -> coumarate) into PAL + C4H (2 steps), providing more mechanistic detail. All intermediates and enzymes match.

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Exact match for the full 7-step pathway: phenylpropanoid -> naringenin -> genistein -> biochanin A. Agent provides the more detailed PAL+C4H route vs GT's PAL/TAL shortcut.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 2 NADPH, 1 ATP, 2 O2, 1 SAM. Correct.

heterologous_recall.notes: All GT pathway enzymes found with functional equivalents: PAL/TAL, 4CL, CHS, CHI, IFS(PlIFS->GmIFS2), HID, OMT(PlOMT9->MtHI4'OMT). Agent additionally includes CPR (valid auxiliary).

ko_recall.notes: GT has no explicit KOs. Agent also has no KOs.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: PlIFS copy number, VHb expression, ER expansion, heme supply. Agent OEs: ARO4, ARO7. Different strategies. Agent focused on precursor supply; GT focused on CYP450 activity enhancement.

source_species_match.breakdown: IFS: G. max vs P. lobata (same domain legume=0.7). OMT: M. truncatula vs P. lobata (same domain legume=0.7). Other enzymes: A. thaliana standard (GT doesn't specify sources for PAL/4CL/CHS/CHI). Average=0.7

step_recall.notes: Agent covers all GT steps with alternatives. GT step 1 (Lac12 import) replaced by de novo lactose synthesis (B4GALT1); GT steps 2-5 all covered. Agent provides more granular breakdown of nucleotide sugar biosynthesis.

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Agent synthesizes lactose de novo from UDP-Gal + glucose (B4GALT1) instead of importing lactose exogenously (Lac12). Both approaches are valid. GT uses lactose as co-substrate; agent achieves it from glucose alone.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: no NADPH/NADH/ATP/O2 for the core glycosylation steps. Correct - glycosyltransferases use UDP-sugar donors.

heterologous_recall.notes: GT heterologous: Lac12 (K. lactis), LgtA (N. meningitidis), LgtB (N. meningitidis). Agent found all three plus B4GALT1 (H. sapiens) for de novo lactose synthesis.

ko_recall.notes: GT has no explicit KOs. Agent proposed GLG1, HUT1, EHT1 KOs for UDP-Glc/UDP-Gal conservation.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: Gal10 (enhanced), GNA1/AGM1/UAP1 (enhanced), LgtA-LgtB tag assembly. Agent found GAL10 OE. Also GFA1 and UGP1 (related but not exact matches). Missed peptide tag assembly strategy.

source_species_match.breakdown: LgtA from N. meningitidis (exact=1.0), LgtB from N. meningitidis (exact=1.0), Lac12 from K. lactis (exact=1.0). B4GALT1 from H. sapiens is extra (not in GT).

step_recall.notes: All 5 GT steps covered. Agent provides more granular detail (8 steps) by separating PAL/C4H/4CL and CHS/CHI into individual reactions. Every GT intermediate and enzyme type is represented.

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Exact match: phenylpropanoid -> naringenin -> genistein -> 3'-hydroxygenistein. Agent identified CYP57B3 from A. oryzae as the 3'-hydroxylase, which is an I3'H enzyme matching GT step 5.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 3 NADPH, 1 ATP, 3 O2. Correct for three CYP450 steps (C4H, IFS, CYP57B3).

heterologous_recall.notes: GT: PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS, CHI, PlIFS, HID, I3'H (8 genes). Agent found all with functional equivalents except uses GmIFS2 instead of PlIFS and CYP57B3 as I3'H. All enzyme types represented. Agent doesn't include separate CPR as it uses native NCP1.

ko_recall.notes: GT has no explicit KOs. Agent proposed ARO10 KO (reasonable).

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: PlIFS copy number, I3'H multi-copy, heme supply, CPR pairing. Agent OEs: ARO7, ZWF1, ACS2, ACC1. Different strategies - agent focused on precursor/cofactor supply; GT focused on CYP450 activity enhancement.

source_species_match.breakdown: IFS: G. max vs P. lobata (same domain legume=0.7). CYP57B3: A. oryzae (fungal I3'H; GT's I3'H source not specified but likely plant=0.4-0.7). Others: standard A. thaliana/M. sativa sources. Average~0.7

step_recall.notes: Agent found the core LDH step (GT step 2) and implicitly covers glycolysis (GT step 1). Missed PDC attenuation (GT step 3, handled via KOs instead) and PfFNT export (GT step 4, significant omission).

pathway_design.path_selection_notes: Core conversion (pyruvate -> L-lactate via LDH) is correct. Missing the PfFNT transporter for efficient lactate export is a significant gap.
pathway_design.cofactor_notes: Agent: 1 NADH consumed. Correct - LDH converts pyruvate + NADH -> L-lactate + NAD+.

heterologous_recall.notes: GT heterologous: LDH, PfFNT (P. falciparum formate-nitrite transporter). Agent found LDH. Missed PfFNT exporter which is a key innovation of the paper.

ko_recall.notes: GT: PDC1/PDC5/PDC6 attenuated. Agent found all three PDC KOs. Agent additionally proposed ADH1/3/5, CYB2, GPD1 KOs which go beyond GT but are biologically reasonable.

oe_recall.notes: GT OEs: PfFNT overexpression (counted as heterologous instead), NADH supply optimization. Agent OE: PYK1 (not in GT).

source_species_match.breakdown: LDH: L. casei (agent) vs unspecified (GT). L. casei is a well-known LDH source. Without GT specifying, assume same_domain=0.7

pathway_design.notes: Step recall ranges from 0.29 (case_003, fundamentally different approach) to 1.0 (cases 006,007,009). Step precision is consistently high (0.71-1.0). 5 out of 10 pathways were exact matches, 4 were reasonable alternatives, 1 was a fundamentally different approach.

engineering_strategy.notes: Heterologous gene precision is perfect (1.0) - all agent-proposed enzymes are biologically valid. Recall varies significantly (0.0 for case_003 where the approach differs, to 1.0 for cases 004/007/008). KO recall is moderate - agent finds the right targets in some cases but not all. OE recall is the weakest dimension (0.24) - the pipeline tends to focus on precursor supply OEs (ARO4/ARO7/ACC1/ZWF1) rather than the paper-specific strategies (CYP450 optimization, peroxisome engineering, enzyme copy number). Source species match averages 0.73, indicating the pipeline typically selects enzymes from the same biological domain (legumes for isoflavonoids, mammals for steroids) even when not matching the exact species.

key_findings: STRENGTH: Pathway reconstruction is strong - the pipeline correctly identifies the core biosynthetic pathway in 9/10 cases, with high step precision (avg 0.97)
STRENGTH: Heterologous gene precision is perfect (1.0) - every enzyme proposed is biologically valid and well-characterized
STRENGTH: Cofactor identification is mostly correct or partially correct

WEAKNESS: OE recall is low (0.24) - the pipeline defaults to generic precursor/cofactor OEs (ARO4/ARO7/ZWF1/ACC1) rather than paper-specific strategies (VHb, ER expansion, PEX engineering, enzyme copy number optimization)
WEAKNESS: The pipeline struggles with novel/non-obvious engineering strategies (PEX peroxisome engineering, mFAS chain-length control, sucrose synthase UDP-Glc supply, PfFNT export)
WEAKNESS: Late-pathway tailoring steps (prenylation, methylation-demethylation cycles) are often missed when they require non-standard KEGG lookups

OBSERVATION: Source species selection is reasonable (avg 0.73) - the pipeline selects from the correct biological domain but tends to use well-characterized model organisms (A. thaliana, G. max) rather than the native source organism
OBSERVATION: KO targets are sometimes alternative but biologically reasonable (e.g., COX10 vs HMX1 for heme degradation, both valid anti-degradation strategies)
